input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets).

---

## Step 1 — Install the .NET SDK

Download the latest .NET SDK (MAUI requires .NET 8+):

```powershell
# Check if dotnet is already installed
dotnet --version

# If not, install via winget:
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
```

## Step 2 — Install MAUI Workloads

```powershell
# List available workloads to find current MAUI ones
dotnet workload search maui

# Install the MAUI workload (includes Android, Windows, iOS stubs)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify what got installed
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 3 — Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows-target MAUI apps need the Windows App SDK and WinUI tooling, which come with VS:

```powershell
# Install VS 2022 Community (or BuildTools) with required workloads
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Then modify the installation to add workloads via CLI:
# (Run from an elevated prompt)
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --includeRecommended --quiet
```

> `NetCrossPlat` = .NET MAUI / mobile. `ManagedDesktop` = WinUI / Windows App SDK.

## Step 4 — Android SDK Setup

The MAUI workload installs a minimal Android SDK. You may need to accept licenses and discover what was installed:

```powershell
# Find where the Android SDK was placed
$androidSdk = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA "Android\Sdk"
if (!(Test-Path $androidSdk)) {
    # Fallback: MAUI sometimes uses the dotnet android SDK path
    $androidSdk = dotnet android sdk path 2>$null
}
Write-Host "Android SDK at: $androidSdk"

# Set the environment variable (persist across sessions)
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidSdk, "User")
$env:ANDROID_HOME = $androidSdk

# Discover installed SDK platforms & build-tools
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list 2>$null |
    Select-String "Installed"

# Accept all licenses
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses

# If cmdline-tools aren't present, install them first
# Download from https://developer.android.com/studio#command-line-tools-only
# Unzip into $androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\
```

### Install specific Android components dynamically:

```powershell
$sdkmanager = "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat"

# Discover latest platform and build-tools versions
& $sdkmanager --list 2>$null | Select-String "platforms;android-" | Select -Last 5
& $sdkmanager --list 2>$null | Select-String "build-tools;" | Select -Last 5

# Install what MAUI needs (adjust versions from output above)
& $sdkmanager "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0" "platform-tools"
```

## Step 5 — Set Up an Android Emulator (optional)

```powershell
$sdkmanager = "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat"
$avdmanager = "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat"

# Install emulator + a system image
& $sdkmanager "emulator" "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create an AVD
& $avdmanager create avd -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"

# Verify Hyper-V / WHPX is enabled (required for emulator acceleration)
Get-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName *Hyper* |
    Select FeatureName, State
```

## Step 6 — Enable Developer Mode (Windows target)

```powershell
# Check current state
reg query "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock"

# Enable developer mode (elevated prompt)
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
    /v AllowDevelopment /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

## Step 7 — Verify Everything

```powershell
# Check all MAUI prerequisites at once
dotnet workload list
dotnet new maui --list

# Create a test project and build for both targets
dotnet new maui -n MauiSmokeTest -o .\MauiSmokeTest
cd MauiSmokeTest

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\emulator\emulator.exe" -avd Pixel_MAUI &
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

## Step 8 — Troubleshooting Commands

```powershell
# Repair workloads if builds fail
dotnet workload repair

# Check which TFMs a project supports
dotnet msbuild -getProperty:TargetFrameworks

# Detailed Android SDK diagnostics
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -v diag 2>&1 | Select-String "AndroidSdk"
```

---

**Key version notes:** Replace `net9.0` with `net8.0` if you installed .NET 8 instead. The Android API level (`android-35`) and build-tools version should match what `--list` reports as latest. The Windows TFM (`10.0.19041.0`) is the minimum; you can target higher.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They can't run commands right now, so they want all the PowerShell commands laid out. Let me provide a comprehensive guide.
